business_problem_summary: A logistics company is processing 3,000+ invoices monthly through a fully manual workflow that requires clerks to open PDFs, extract line items, cross-reference purchase orders in SAP S/4HANA, and manually flag discrepancies. This consumes two full-time employees, produces a 4% error rate, and causes downstream payment delays and vendor relationship friction. The root cause is the absence of a structured document ingestion and validation pipeline — basic OCR tools failed due to inconsistent PDF layouts across the vendor base.
operational_pain_points: Two full-time clerks spend 100% of their working hours manually processing 3,000 invoices per month — a task that delivers no strategic value.
A 4% error rate on invoice extraction causes downstream payment delays averaging 3–5 days per affected vendor.
Existing OCR tools fail on 30–40% of invoices due to inconsistent vendor PDF layouts, requiring full manual re-processing.
There is no audit trail for manual decisions, making vendor dispute resolution slow and evidence-free.
Invoice processing backlog peaks at month-end, forcing overtime and creating cash flow forecasting uncertainty.

ai_opportunity_classification.title: Intelligent Document Extraction
ai_opportunity_classification.description: Use a vision-capable LLM to extract structured line-item data from heterogeneous PDF invoices regardless of layout, handling both native and scanned documents.

ai_opportunity_classification.title: Automated PO Matching
ai_opportunity_classification.description: Match extracted invoice data against SAP purchase orders using fuzzy matching and configurable business rule validation to flag discrepancies automatically.

ai_opportunity_classification.title: Exception Routing and Human-in-the-Loop Review
ai_opportunity_classification.description: Route low-confidence extractions and unresolved discrepancies to a human review queue, reducing clerk workload to exception handling only.

suggested_architecture: A hybrid OCR + API Integration architecture built around three components: (1) A document ingestion pipeline using a vision-capable LLM to extract structured data from PDF invoices, handling variable layouts and scanned documents with a confidence score per field. (2) A validation layer that calls the SAP S/4HANA REST API to match extracted data against open purchase orders, applying configurable tolerance thresholds for amounts and dates. (3) An exception management queue where low-confidence items are routed for human review via a lightweight web interface. The pipeline runs asynchronously, stores an audit log of every decision, and integrates with SAP via its standard OData REST APIs. Infrastructure is containerized and deployable to any cloud provider.

agentic_workflow.action: Receive uploaded PDF invoice and extract raw text and page structure
agentic_workflow.input: Raw PDF file from upload endpoint
agentic_workflow.output: Structured text with page layout metadata

agentic_workflow.action: Parse extracted text into structured invoice fields with per-field confidence scores
agentic_workflow.input: Structured text from Document Ingestion Agent
agentic_workflow.output: JSON with vendor ID, PO number, line items, unit prices, totals, and invoice date

agentic_workflow.action: Cross-reference extracted invoice fields against SAP open purchase orders
agentic_workflow.input: Structured invoice JSON from Field Extraction Agent
agentic_workflow.output: Match result with overall confidence score and itemised discrepancy list

agentic_workflow.action: Auto-approve high-confidence matches or route exceptions to the human review queue
agentic_workflow.input: Match result and confidence score from PO Matching Agent
agentic_workflow.output: Approved invoice record written to SAP, or flagged exception ticket in review queue

data_requirements.description: Monthly intake of 3,000+ invoices in heterogeneous PDF formats — both native digital and scanned. Required as the primary input to the extraction pipeline.
data_requirements.notes: Layouts vary significantly across the vendor base. A labelled sample of at least 200 invoices from the top 20 vendors by volume is required for model evaluation before go-live.

data_requirements.description: Open and partially-delivered PO records used to validate extracted invoice data against approved purchase commitments.
data_requirements.notes: API access requires a security approval from the SAP basis team — estimate 1–2 weeks lead time. A CSV export must be available as an interim fallback to unblock pipeline development.

data_requirements.description: Vendor IDs, common name aliases, and standard payment terms used to normalise vendor name extraction and resolve matching ambiguities.
data_requirements.notes: Available in SAP. Export once at project start and refresh monthly. No PII concerns.

integration_points.description: Bidirectional: read open POs for invoice matching; write approved invoice records and trigger payment workflows back to SAP via OData REST APIs.
Inbound: receive vendor invoice attachments via a monitored mailbox and trigger the ingestion pipeline automatically on arrival.
Internal lightweight web application where clerks review flagged invoices, inspect per-field confidence scores, override AI decisions, and approve or reject.

engineering_backlog.title: Project scaffold and CI pipeline
engineering_backlog.description: Initialise repository, configure GitHub Actions with lint and test jobs, set up Docker Compose for local development. Ensures a clean foundation before feature work begins.

engineering_backlog.title: PDF ingestion and OCR extraction service
engineering_backlog.description: Build document ingestion service supporting native PDF text extraction and a vision-model fallback for scanned documents. Return structured text with page metadata.

engineering_backlog.title: LLM field extraction with confidence scoring
engineering_backlog.description: Design and iterate prompt templates for extracting vendor, PO number, line items, and totals. Add Pydantic validation and assign a confidence score (0–1) to each extracted field.

engineering_backlog.title: SAP S/4HANA OData API integration
engineering_backlog.description: Implement an authenticated REST client for SAP PO retrieval. Map extracted invoice fields to the SAP data model. Include error handling for auth failures and timeout.

engineering_backlog.title: Exception review queue UI
engineering_backlog.description: Build a lightweight web interface for clerks to review flagged invoices, inspect field-level confidence scores, override decisions, and approve or reject.

engineering_backlog.title: Audit log and reporting dashboard
engineering_backlog.description: Store all processing results with timestamps and decision rationale. Build a dashboard showing daily throughput, auto-approval rate, error rate, and estimated cost savings.

risks_and_assumptions.description: Invoice PDF formats vary significantly across the vendor base, including low-quality scans that degrade OCR accuracy.
risks_and_assumptions.mitigation: Implement field-level confidence scoring; route low-confidence extractions to human review. Build vendor-specific extraction profiles for the top 20 vendors by volume.

risks_and_assumptions.description: SAP S/4HANA API access may require security approvals and custom configuration that extends the integration timeline.
risks_and_assumptions.mitigation: Initiate SAP API onboarding in week 1. Define a CSV export fallback as an interim integration method to unblock pipeline development.

risks_and_assumptions.description: Clerks may distrust auto-approval decisions or resist the new exception review workflow.
risks_and_assumptions.mitigation: Involve one clerk in UAT from week 3. Run the new system in parallel with the manual process for two weeks before full cutover.

risks_and_assumptions.description: LLM extraction accuracy may require multiple prompt iteration cycles before reaching the 95% threshold needed for production.
risks_and_assumptions.mitigation: Reserve week 3 for prompt engineering and accuracy benchmarking against a labelled set of 200 representative invoices.

testing_plan.description: Test field extraction logic against 50 labelled invoice samples covering the top vendor PDF formats.
testing_plan.expected_outcome: Field-level extraction accuracy >= 95% on the test set

testing_plan.description: Test the end-to-end pipeline from PDF upload to SAP match result using mocked SAP API responses.
testing_plan.expected_outcome: Pipeline completes without errors for all three invoice types: native PDF, scanned, and mixed-layout

testing_plan.description: Process 100 real invoices from the prior month in a staging environment connected to the SAP sandbox.
testing_plan.expected_outcome: Auto-approval rate >= 80%; discrepancy detection results match manual review outcomes

testing_plan.description: Clerk UAT: two clerks process a batch of 30 invoices using the new exception review interface.
testing_plan.expected_outcome: Task completion time reduced by >= 60%; no critical usability issues reported by either clerk

executive_summary: Your team currently processes 3,000 invoices per month manually — a workflow that occupies two full-time employees, produces a 4% error rate, and generates recurring vendor payment delays. This proposal replaces that manual workflow with an AI-powered invoice processing system that automatically reads any PDF format, validates data against your SAP purchase orders, and routes only exceptions to your team for review. Based on comparable deployments in logistics, you can expect to reduce invoice processing time by 70–80%, cut errors to under 0.5%, and free your clerks to focus on vendor relationships and dispute resolution. The system integrates with your existing SAP environment, runs on standard cloud infrastructure, and can be operational within 8 weeks. Estimated implementation investment: $60,000–$80,000. Estimated annual savings: $180,000–$220,000 in labour and error-related costs — a payback period of under five months.

delivery_status_report.title: Foundation
deliverables: Sample invoice dataset collected and labelled (50 invoices)

delivery_status_report.title: Core Extraction Pipeline
deliverables: PDF ingestion service handling native and scanned documents
Field extraction with LLM working on native PDFs
Initial accuracy benchmark: >= 80% on labelled set

delivery_status_report.title: Accuracy Tuning and SAP Integration
deliverables: SAP PO matching service integrated and passing integration tests

delivery_status_report.title: Review UI, UAT, and Parallel Run
deliverables: Exception review UI deployed to staging
Parallel run with manual process initiated — monitoring auto-approval rate
